content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Path Queries". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/1213/problem/G
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then for each test case: The first line contains two integers n and q, the number of nodes and the number of queries. The next line contains n-1 integers representing the edges of the tree. The next q lines contain two integers u and v, representing the queries.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print the number of nodes on the path between u and v.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use Depth-First Search (DFS) to find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of each query pair and calculate the path length.
